MINI MINUTES: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday December 2, 2025, AT 3:30 PM in Department 304.
No appearances, not contested, The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

The Application of Plaintiff Janiss Miller as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Stanley Miller and of Plaintiff Zachary Miller as Wrongful Death Heir of Stanley Miller for Entry of Default Against Defendant Westward Dental Products Co. in the amount of $3,118,550.25 is GRANTED.

The Court has reviewed the prove-up packet including the Judicial Council Form Civ-100, the memorandum, Mr. Nevin's declaration and the attached exhibits, which are admitted. The Court finds Defendant has been served with process, failed to answer or appear and defend the action within the time allowed by law, and default has been duly entered. As such, Plaintiffs are entitled to the relief sought in the Application. The Court intends to execute the proposed judgment accompanying the application.

Off Calendar Feb-20-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, not contested, and the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Order Substituting Janiss Miller as Successor-in-Interest to and for Deceased Plaintiff; for Leave to File Second Amended Complaint; and to Add Previously Sued Doe Defendants to the Second Amended Complaint is GRANTED under C.C.P. sections 377.20, 377.31, 377.11, 377.32, 377.60, and 473. No opposition filed.

As of the date of the granting of Petitioner's motion, the Second Amended Complaint is deemed served on all Defendants who have previously appeared. (previously sued as Doe 3); (4) Metropolitan Life Insurance (sued as Does 4, 451, and 750); and (5) LLT Management LLC (formerly known as LTL Management LLC), a subsidiary of Johnson & Johnson (previously sued as Does 5 and 791).

The prevailing party must prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and submit it to Department 301.
